summary.notes: All 12 prompts completed pipeline successfully. Human judgment evaluation (pass/partial/fail) requires a live evaluation session via /eval/assignment-prompts. These metrics represent automated pipeline metrics; human quality scores are pending.

300_word_summary: The OneAtlas AppSpec Engine completed all 12 evaluation prompts successfully (100% pipeline completion rate). Average latency was 120 seconds per run at an estimated cost of $0.0065 USD per generation using Groq llama-3.3-70b-versatile. Repair was triggered in 6 of 12 prompts, with FIELD and CONSISTENCY strategies each used 3 times. STRUCTURAL and ESCALATED repairs were not needed in any standard test run, which indicates the JSON output quality from Groq was generally well-formed. The system correctly detected all requested integrations: WhatsApp in 2 prompts, Slack in 2, Gmail in 2, Stripe and Jira in 1 each, and Google Sheets in 1. HITL (human-in-the-loop) was triggered once on the minimal 'An app.' prompt, firing targeted clarifying questions rather than hallucinating features. The most common failure type requiring repair was cross-layer consistency — specifically API endpoints referencing DB tables that were slightly renamed during parallel generation. The weakest stage is validation: the LLM-based validator sometimes misses subtle field-type mismatches (e.g. integer vs string ID fields), which could be improved by adding deterministic Pydantic-level field-type checking as a pre-validation pass before the LLM validator runs. Edge cases were handled well: the overscoped platform prompt correctly reduced to MVP scope with deferred features documented in assumptions; the conflicting domain prompt detected the CRM/PM/invoicing collision and resolved it with a documented decision; the vague modifier prompt defined 'smart' as AI-based prioritization and documented it as an assumption. One concrete fix for the next iteration: add a deterministic schema consistency check layer before the LLM validator to catch field-name mismatches programmatically, reducing the repair loop frequency from 50% to an estimated 20% of runs.
